- question storage and reuse would be inconsistent,
- and the platform would not have a scalable foundation for future Assessment growth.

---

## 6. Objectives

The objectives of this Epic are:

- Establish Assessment as the canonical terminology and entity in the Tools module.
- Enable SuperAdmin to create and manage Assessments for a tenant.
- Support Assessment metadata such as short description, long description, context, and participant benefit.
- Support multiple reusable Assessment render styles.
- Store an Assessment-specific AI analysis prompt for result interpretation.
- Optionally store an Assessment-specific AI question-generation prompt for authoring support.
- Store question banks against an Assessment rather than hardcoding questions into page components.
- Allow the platform to fetch only the configured number of questions for a given Assessment attempt.
- Support AI-generated narrative reports through Groq, Gemini, or equivalent backend AI services.
- Integrate Assessments with assignment workflows, report delivery, and future widget-based usage.
- Future-proof the module for richer gamification and creator-led Assessment authoring.

---

## 7. Non-Objectives

The following are explicitly out of scope for this Epic:

- Creating 12 to 20 custom-coded pages for each individual Assessment
- Fully dynamic live question generation during participant runtime
- Advanced psychometric calibration and norming
- Adaptive testing that changes difficulty based on prior answers
- Assessment marketplace pricing and monetization rules
- Enterprise benchmarking dashboards
- Full multilingual localization in the first release
- Real-time multiplayer assessment experiences
- Fully autonomous, unreviewed AI-generated question delivery at runtime

---

## 8. In-Scope Capabilities

This Epic includes the following capabilities:

- SuperAdmin creates and manages Assessment definitions
- Assessment metadata is stored in the datastore
- Questions are associated with an Assessment through Assessment ID
- Assessment-specific AI analysis prompts are stored
- Optional Assessment-specific AI question-generation prompts are stored
- Runtime fetches the configured number of questions for an Assessment attempt
- Assessment delivery is driven by metadata and render style
- The frontend uses reusable render-style layouts or components instead of one custom page per Assessment
- Participant responses are captured and stored
- Structured result data is sent to backend AI analysis services
- AI-generated report output is stored and made available to users
- Assessment completion can integrate with assignment state transitions

---

## 9. Out-of-Scope Capabilities

This Epic does not include:

- live collaborative assessment authoring by multiple admins,
- question marketplace and cross-tenant sharing,
- advanced adaptive branching engines,
- fully autonomous AI-generated question sets shown without review,
- deep psychometric scoring models,
- and multiplayer gamified assessment formats.

---

## 10. Domain Definitions

### 10.1 Tool
A Tool is a functional interactive resource within StudioVerse that allows a user to provide input and receive a structured result, outcome, or insight.

### 10.2 Assessment
An Assessment is a type of Tool that presents a structured set of questions or interactive tasks to a participant in order to evaluate awareness, capability, preferences, behavior, readiness, or other development-related dimensions.

### 10.3 Assessment Question Bank
A Question Bank is the collection of approved questions associated with a specific Assessment and stored against that Assessment for runtime selection.

### 10.4 Assessment Attempt
An Assessment Attempt is a single participant interaction session in which the platform fetches questions, records answers, calculates raw outputs, and generates or requests a final report.

### 10.5 AI Analysis Prompt
An AI Analysis Prompt is the Assessment-specific instruction template used by the backend AI service to interpret submitted answers and generate a meaningful narrative report.

### 10.6 AI Question Generation Prompt
An AI Question Generation Prompt is an optional Assessment-specific instruction template used during admin authoring to generate draft question suggestions for review and storage.

### 10.7 Render Style
Render Style defines how the Assessment is visually presented and how the participant interacts with the Assessment on the screen.

---

## 11. User Roles

### 11.1 SuperAdmin
The SuperAdmin can create, edit, configure, publish, and govern Assessments and their associated Question Banks across the tenant.

### 11.2 Professional
A Professional can assign Assessments, review outputs, and use reports to guide sessions or interventions. In future phases, Professionals may also create their own Assessments subject to governance rules.

### 11.3 Individual
An Individual is the participant who takes the Assessment and receives the resulting report or insight output.

---

## 12. Functional Requirements

### 12.1 Assessment Creation
The system shall allow the SuperAdmin to create an Assessment for a selected tenant.

The Assessment creation flow shall support capture of:
- Assessment name
- Assessment short description
- Assessment long description
- Assessment context
- Assessment benefit
- Assessment type
- Render style
- Number of questions in bank
- Number of questions to present per attempt
- AI analysis prompt
- Optional AI question-generation prompt
- Status
- Publication state
- Ownership metadata

### 12.2 Assessment Terminology
The platform shall use the term **Assessment** in product language, documentation, admin forms, participant-facing screens, and system metadata rather than the term Test.

### 12.3 Assessment Context Fields
Each Assessment shall support richer descriptive metadata so that both the participant experience and the AI interpretation remain grounded in context and purpose rather than only raw scores.

At minimum, the system shall store:
- short description,
- long description,
- Assessment context,
- and participant benefit.

### 12.4 Question Bank Association
Each Assessment shall support an associated Question Bank stored in the datastore and linked by `assessmentId` rather than hardcoded into page components.

### 12.5 Question Count Configuration
The system shall allow each Assessment to define:
- total question count available in the bank,
- and the number of questions to be fetched and presented for a participant attempt.

The system shall not require all stored questions to be displayed in every attempt.

### 12.6 Runtime Delivery
At runtime, the system shall:
- load Assessment metadata,
- fetch the configured set of questions,
- choose the correct render style,
- display the questions using the appropriate reusable renderer,
- collect participant responses,
- and submit the result payload for scoring and AI interpretation.

### 12.7 AI Analysis Prompt
Each Assessment shall store an Assessment-specific AI analysis prompt used after submission to interpret the participant’s responses and generate the report.

### 12.8 AI Question Generation Prompt
The system should optionally support a separate AI prompt for generating draft questions during Assessment authoring so that question creation and answer interpretation remain separate concerns.

### 12.9 Reusable Renderers
The frontend shall use a reusable renderer model rather than a separate custom page for every Assessment.

This means the platform shall maintain a small number of reusable render-style pages or components and use metadata to decide which one to render for a given Assessment.

### 12.10 Results and Reports
After an Assessment Attempt is completed, the system shall:
- store the participant’s submitted answers,
- produce raw result data,
- send structured Assessment context plus answers to the configured AI provider,
- receive AI analysis,
- create a report record,
- and make the report available to the appropriate users.

---

## 13. Supported Render Styles

The first release of the Assessment Engine shall support the following render styles.

### 13.1 Single-Choice Quiz
A participant sees a question and selects one answer from multiple radio-button options.

### 13.2 Instant-Feedback Multi-Choice
A participant sees multiple options and receives immediate green or red feedback when selecting an answer, creating a more interactive and game-like experience.

### 13.3 Select-and-Move / Select-All-That-Apply
A participant sees a list of items on one side and selects items which move to another area, visually representing chosen responses.

### 13.4 Image-Based Single-Choice
A participant sees an image, supporting descriptive text or scenario text, and then answers a single-choice question based on that visual context.

### 13.5 Gamified Style
The platform should support a fifth, more gamified style. The preferred candidate is a drag-and-drop matching or ordering interaction because it is interactive, intuitive, and still manageable in an MVP architecture.

### 13.6 Future Render Styles
The engine shall be designed so that future render styles can be added without redesigning the whole Assessment model.

---

## 14. Data Requirements

### 14.1 Assessment Entity
The Assessment entity shall support at minimum the following fields:

- `id`
- `tenantId`
- `name`
- `shortDescription`
- `longDescription`
- `assessmentContext`
- `assessmentBenefit`
- `assessmentType`
- `renderStyle`
- `questionBankCount`
- `questionsPerAttempt`
- `analysisPrompt`
- `questionGenerationPrompt`
- `status`
- `publicationState`
- `ownershipScope`
- `ownerEntityId`
- `createdBy`
- `updatedBy`
- `createdAt`
- `updatedAt`
- `publishedAt`

### 14.2 Assessment Question Entity
The Assessment Question entity shall support at minimum:

- `id`
- `assessmentId`
- `tenantId`
- `questionText`
- `questionType`
- `renderStyle`
- `options`
- `correctAnswer`
- `scoringRule`
- `imageUrl`
- `imageDescription`
- `displayOrder`
- `weight`
- `tags`
- `isActive`
- `createdAt`
- `updatedAt`

### 14.3 Assessment Attempt Entity
The Assessment Attempt entity shall support at minimum:

- `id`
- `assessmentId`
- `tenantId`
- `userId`
- `assignmentId`
- `questionsServed`
- `answersSubmitted`
- `rawScore`
- `rawResultPayload`
- `status`
- `startedAt`
- `completedAt`

### 14.4 Assessment Report Entity
The Assessment Report entity shall support at minimum:

- `id`
- `assessmentId`
- `attemptId`
- `tenantId`
- `userId`
- `aiProvider`
- `analysisPromptUsed`
- `aiResponseRaw`
- `reportSummary`
- `reportStructuredData`
- `pdfUrl`
- `createdAt`

### 14.5 Modeling Guidance
Implementation may begin with separate collections for:
- `assessments`
- `assessmentQuestions`
- `assessmentAttempts`
- `assessmentReports`

This is preferred over hardcoding questions in UI code or embedding large question sets directly into route-level components.

---

## 15. Business Rules

- The platform shall use **Assessment** as the official term for this capability.
- An Assessment must belong to one tenant.
- Questions must be associated with an Assessment by `assessmentId`.
- Questions must not be hardcoded into 12 to 20 individual frontend pages.
- The number of questions shown in an attempt may be fewer than the number stored in the Question Bank.
- AI analysis prompts must be stored per Assessment, not as one global prompt for all Assessments.
- AI question-generation prompts and AI analysis prompts should be treated as separate concerns.
- Runtime Assessment behavior must be chosen from metadata rather than custom code per Assessment.
- External AI model calls must happen from backend services only, not from the frontend.
- Assessment completion should support assignment completion workflows when the Assessment was assigned through the platform.

---

## 16. Architectural Recommendation

The platform should **not** create separate `page.tsx` files for every Assessment. That approach may seem fast initially but will create duplication, inconsistency, and high maintenance overhead as the number of Assessments grows.

Instead, the recommended architecture is:
- one generic Assessment runtime route,
- a small set of reusable renderer components or pages,
- metadata-driven renderer selection,
- separate Assessment and Question storage,
- and backend-driven scoring and AI analysis.

This approach is more scalable, more consistent, easier to test, and better aligned with StudioVerse’s configuration-driven product philosophy.

---

## 17. AI Analysis Model

Each Assessment shall carry a dedicated AI analysis prompt because the meaning of the participant’s answers depends on the specific purpose of the Assessment.

For example, an Assessment focused on self-awareness requires a different interpretive lens than an Assessment focused on decision-making, leadership style, blind spots, or developmental readiness.

Therefore, the system must send not only answers and scores, but also the Assessment context, benefit, and prompt instructions when generating the final report.

The platform should also support provider abstraction so that report generation may be performed by Groq, Gemini, or equivalent approved AI services without changing the Assessment model.

---

## 18. User Stories

### US-E4-01 — Define Assessment Domain Model
**As a** developer,  
**I want** a canonical Assessment model,  
**so that** all Assessments use one stable structure.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Assessment terminology is used consistently.
- Metadata fields include short description, long description, context, and benefit.
- Assessment supports render style and AI prompt fields.
- Schema supports linked Question Banks and reports.

---

### US-E4-02 — SuperAdmin Can Create an Assessment
**As a** SuperAdmin,  
**I want** to create an Assessment definition,  
**so that** the platform can deliver it to users.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- SuperAdmin can create a draft Assessment.
- Required metadata can be stored.
- AI analysis prompt can be stored.
- Assessment belongs to the selected tenant.

---

### US-E4-03 — SuperAdmin Can Associate Questions to an Assessment
**As a** SuperAdmin,  
**I want** to add questions to an Assessment,  
**so that** the Assessment can be delivered dynamically.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Questions are stored against `assessmentId`.
- Questions are not embedded in UI code.
- Questions can support different interaction styles.

---

### US-E4-04 — Runtime Chooses the Correct Render Style
**As a** participant,  
**I want** the Assessment to display correctly based on its interaction pattern,  
**so that** the experience feels intuitive.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Runtime reads render-style metadata.
- Correct renderer is selected.
- The same renderer can be reused across multiple Assessments.

---

### US-E4-05 — Platform Fetches Configured Number of Questions
**As a** Product Owner,  
**I want** each Assessment to control how many questions appear per attempt,  
**so that** the platform can support both fixed and subset-driven delivery.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Assessment stores `questionsPerAttempt`.
- Runtime fetches only the configured number.
- The Question Bank can contain more questions than the attempt displays.

---

### US-E4-06 — Platform Generates Assessment-Specific AI Report
**As a** Professional,  
**I want** submitted Assessment answers to be analyzed in the right context,  
**so that** the resulting report is meaningful and useful.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Assessment-specific AI prompt is used.
- Structured answers are sent to backend AI services.
- AI response is stored with the report record.

---

### US-E4-07 — Assessment Completion Updates Assignment
**As a** Professional,  
**I want** assigned Assessments to update completion status when finished,  
**so that** assignment tracking remains accurate.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- If an Assessment is assignment-linked, completion updates assignment state.
- Report generation can happen after submission.
- Appropriate users can access the output.

---

## 19. QA Considerations

QA should validate the following scenarios:

- Assessment creation with all required metadata
- Correct storage of short description, long description, context, and benefit
- Question association by `assessmentId`
- Correct fetch count for `questionsPerAttempt`
- Correct renderer selection based on render-style metadata
- Single-choice flow behavior
- Instant-feedback flow behavior
- Select-and-move flow behavior
- Image-based flow behavior
- Gamified flow behavior where supported
- Answer persistence
- Backend AI prompt selection
- Report creation
- Assignment completion updates
- Tenant isolation across all Assessment records

---

## 20. Integration Considerations

This Epic is expected to integrate with:
- the Assignments engine for delivery and completion tracking,
- the Reports engine for AI-generated outputs and PDFs,
- Notifications for report delivery,
- Widgets for embeddable external Assessment entry points,
- and future analytics services for longitudinal insight tracking.

The implementation should ensure that all AI calls occur in backend services or functions, not in the frontend.

---

## 21. Dependencies

This Epic depends on:
- authenticated platform access,
- tenant-aware authorization,
- datastore persistence,
- backend service or function layer for business logic,
- AI provider integration,
- report-generation capability,
- and assignment/report module interoperability.

This Epic may later be extended by:
- Assessment Widget Publishing Epic
- Assessment Marketplace Epic
- Professional-Created Assessment Epic
- Assessment Benchmarking and Analytics Epic
- Advanced Gamification Epic

---

## 22. Risks and Considerations

- If each Assessment is custom-built as a separate page, maintenance cost will rise quickly and the engine will not scale.
- If AI prompts are global rather than Assessment-specific, report quality will decline because the analysis will ignore Assessment context.
- If question-generation prompts and analysis prompts are not separated, the platform will mix authoring concerns with interpretation concerns.
- If questions are generated live at runtime without prior review, content quality and reliability may become inconsistent.
- If render styles are not modeled explicitly, frontend complexity will increase and reuse will decrease.

---

## 23. Definition of Done

This Epic is considered complete when:

- Assessment is implemented as the canonical Tools terminology.
- SuperAdmin can create and manage Assessments.
- Assessment metadata includes short description, long description, context, and benefit.
- Questions are stored separately and linked to the Assessment.
- Runtime fetches the configured number of questions.
- Assessments render correctly through reusable style-based components.
- Assessment-specific AI prompts are stored and used.
- Completed Attempts generate reports through backend AI workflows.
- Assignment-linked Assessments update assignment status appropriately.
- Product, Engineering, and QA approve the implementation behavior.

---

## 24. Copilot Implementation Note

This document may be used as an implementation specification for GitHub Copilot or other AI coding tools.

When using this document as a coding reference:
- treat this file as the source of truth for Assessment terminology and structure,
- do not create one custom page per Assessment,
- implement a metadata-driven Assessment runtime,
- store Questions separately from renderer code,
- keep AI question generation and AI analysis as separate concerns,
- and ensure all AI model invocation is done through backend services or functions.

---

This PRD is the source document for:

**Document Type:** Functional Product Requirements Document

> Use "Wallet", "Coins", "Manage Wallet", and "My Wallet" consistently.
> Keep the system tenant-aware and ledger-driven.

## Implementation status snapshot — April 12, 2026

- SuperAdmin now uses **Manage Wallet** as the canonical navigation label.
- The SuperAdmin Manage Wallet page lists existing wallets first, supports user-type radio filtering, supports wallet creation for a selected user, and supports coin issuance.
- Logged-in users have a dedicated **Manage Wallet** page that shows wallet summary plus transaction history.
- Assignment-related debits are written to wallet transactions and should be visible from the spender's wallet history.

## Implementation status snapshot — April 19, 2026 (coin request lifecycle + security enforcement)

- Initial wallet issuance (10 coins) is now applied automatically to newly scoped users at creation time, ensuring wallet readiness eliminates onboarding friction.
- Professional coin request lifecycle is fully implemented: Professionals can request coins from their associated Company via `/<tenant>/request-coins`.
- Company users see pending coin request count as a live badge on the Manage Wallet page, refreshing when requests are approved or denied.
- Firestore security rules enforce coin request governance: Professionals can create requests, Companies can approve/deny, and access is scoped by company association.
- Coin request routing uses profile association context (Professional.associatedCompanyId) to ensure requests reach the correct approver.
- Wallet pages (Manage Wallet, Buy Coins, Request Coins) are available cross-tenant under `/<tenant>/manage-wallet`, `/<tenant>/buy-coins`, and `/<tenant>/request-coins`.

---

## 1. Purpose

This document defines the business and functional requirements for the **Manage Wallet** capability in StudioVerse.

The purpose of this Epic is to allow the **SuperAdmin** to issue platform coins to tenant entities and end users, and allow recipients to see their available balance within **My Wallet** after login.

This Epic also establishes the administrative controls, wallet visibility model, transaction tracking, dashboard summary behavior, and future-ready ledger structure required for controlled coin-based access and usage across the StudioVerse platform.

---

## 2. Audience

### Business Stakeholders
This document explains how coins act as a governed internal value mechanism that can be issued centrally, distributed to different user types, and later consumed through platform activity.

### Product Owners / Product Management
This document defines the scope, terminology, role-based behavior, wallet data model, issuance flow, dashboard summary rules, and future extensibility for Manage Wallet.

### Engineering Teams
This document provides the functional requirements needed to implement wallet balances, coin issuance workflows, transaction ledgers, admin controls, and dashboard summary metrics in a scalable multi-tenant structure.

### QA Teams
This document provides the basis for validating coin issuance, role-based assignment, wallet visibility, dashboard totals, unused vs utilized calculations, and tenant-level correctness.

### Design Teams
This document describes the user-facing wallet experiences, SuperAdmin issuance flow, and dashboard entry points that must be intuitive and role-aware.

---

## 3. Executive Summary

StudioVerse requires a platform-native mechanism to issue and track **coins** that can be assigned by the SuperAdmin to Companies, Coaches / Professionals, or Individuals.

Once coins are issued, the logged-in recipient must be able to see their balance in **My Wallet**, creating a direct and transparent connection between administrative allocation and user-level visibility.

The SuperAdmin must also have a dedicated management entry point called **Manage Wallet**, where wallets can be reviewed and coins can be assigned after selecting the tenant and the target user type.

In addition, the SuperAdmin dashboard must contain a summary tile that shows overall platform utilization in a form such as **10/20**, where:

- **10** = total utilized coins
- **20** = total issued coins across all tenants

Selecting that tile must take the SuperAdmin directly to the Manage Wallet page.

---

## 4. Business Context

As StudioVerse evolves into a structured multi-tenant platform with centrally governed experiences, it requires an internal allocation model that allows the platform owner to control usage and entitlement in a measurable way.

Coins provide that mechanism. They can represent access value, usage credits, or controlled platform entitlement units that the SuperAdmin can allocate across the ecosystem.

This model is especially valuable in a multi-tenant setup because allocation may need to happen at more than one level:

- to a Company / Tenant,
- to a Coach / Professional,
- or directly to an Individual user.

---

## 5. Problem Statement

StudioVerse currently needs a structured Wallet capability that allows coins to be issued, surfaced to users, and tracked consistently across the platform.

Without this capability:

- there is no canonical place to assign coins,
- users cannot clearly see what has been allocated to them,
- administrators cannot track how many coins have been issued or used,
- and the platform lacks the ledger foundation needed for future controlled consumption use cases.

The platform also requires a clear separation between:

- **coin issuance**,
- **coin visibility**, and
- **coin utilization**,

so that the system can accurately report assigned balance versus actual usage over time.

---

## 6. Objectives

The objectives of this Epic are:

- Enable SuperAdmin to assign coins to a Company, Coach / Professional, or Individual.
- Allow logged-in users to see their coin balance in **My Wallet**.
- Add a dedicated **Manage Wallet** menu item to the SuperAdmin navigation.
- Allow the SuperAdmin to choose a tenant before assigning coins.
- Allow the SuperAdmin to choose a target user type before assigning coins.
- Record every allocation in a transaction-safe ledger structure.
- Track total issued coins across all tenants.
- Track utilized coins separately from issued coins.
- Show a dashboard tile summarizing utilized vs issued totals in a format such as 10/20.
- Allow the dashboard tile to deep-link to the Manage Wallet page.
- Future-proof the system for controlled debit / consumption scenarios.

---

## 7. Non-Objectives

The following are out of scope for this Epic:

- external payment gateway integration,
- purchase of coins by end customers,
- invoicing and taxation,
- refund workflows,
- exchange rates or real-money conversion,
- promotional coupon campaigns,
- coin transfer between users,
- and advanced finance reporting.

This Epic is focused on **internal administrative issuance and wallet visibility**, not public commerce.

---

## 8. In-Scope Capabilities

This Epic includes the following capabilities:

- SuperAdmin can assign coins.
- Coin assignment can target Company / Tenant, Coach / Professional, or Individual.
- Target entity selection is tenant-aware.
- Wallet balances are visible to the logged-in user in **My Wallet**.
- SuperAdmin dashboard shows utilized vs issued counts.
- Dashboard tile navigates to Manage Coins.
- All issuance records are stored in a wallet ledger or transaction model.
- The system supports unused balance calculation.
- The system is structured for future debit / consumption logic.

---

## 9. Out-of-Scope Capabilities

This Epic does not include:

- direct end-user coin purchases,
- peer-to-peer transfers,
- subscription billing tied to coins,
- bulk CSV import of coin assignments,
- accounting exports,
- and marketplace commerce logic.

---

## 10. Domain Definitions

### 10.1 Coin
A Coin is an internal StudioVerse value unit issued by the platform for controlled entitlement, balance tracking, and future usage consumption.

### 10.2 Wallet
A Wallet is the system-managed balance container through which a logged-in user or entity can view issued, used, and available coin values.

### 10.3 Coin Issuance
Coin Issuance is the action by which the SuperAdmin allocates a specified number of coins to a valid target entity.

### 10.4 Wallet Transaction
A Wallet Transaction is a ledger record representing a balance-affecting event such as issue, adjustment, debit, reversal, or expiry.

### 10.5 Utilized Coins
Utilized Coins are coins that have already been consumed by a valid platform action.

### 10.6 Unused Coins
Unused Coins are coins that have been issued but not yet utilized.

### 10.7 Target User Type
The Target User Type defines who receives the coin allocation:
- Company / Tenant
- Coach / Professional
- Individual

---

## 11. User Roles

### 11.1 SuperAdmin
The SuperAdmin can issue coins, review allocation data, and track issued versus utilized totals across the platform.

### 11.2 Company / Tenant
A Company may receive coins through the SuperAdmin allocation flow and may later use those coins for platform-controlled activity.

### 11.3 Coach / Professional
A Coach / Professional may receive coins directly through the SuperAdmin flow and must be able to view them in My Wallet once logged in.

### 11.4 Individual
An Individual may receive coins directly and must be able to see the assigned balance in My Wallet after login.

---

## 12. Functional Requirements

### 12.1 SuperAdmin Navigation
The system shall add a new menu item for SuperAdmin called **Manage Wallet**.

### 12.2 Dashboard Tile
The SuperAdmin dashboard shall contain a tile showing:
- total utilized coins,
- total issued coins,
- and a compact ratio-style summary such as **10/20**.

The tile shall be clickable and shall navigate the user to the **Manage Wallet** page.

### 12.3 Manage Wallet Page
The system shall provide a SuperAdmin-only page for wallet management.

The page shall support:
- existing wallet listing,
- user-type radio filtering,
- tenant selection,
- target user type selection,
- target entity lookup / selection,
- explicit wallet creation for a selected user when no wallet exists,
- coin quantity entry,
- issuance action,
- and recent allocation / debit history.

### 12.4 Tenant Selection
Before assigning coins, the SuperAdmin shall be able to select the target tenant.

### 12.5 Target Type Selection
After selecting the tenant, the SuperAdmin shall be able to choose the type of recipient:
- Company / Tenant
- Coach / Professional
- Individual

### 12.6 Target Entity Selection
Once the target type is selected, the system shall present valid entities from that tenant for selection, except in the case of a Company-level allocation where the tenant itself may be the target entity.

### 12.7 Coin Assignment
The SuperAdmin shall be able to assign a positive integer number of coins to the selected target entity.

### 12.8 Wallet Visibility
When coins are assigned to a user-level target, the logged-in recipient shall be able to see those coins in **Manage Wallet / My Wallet**.

### 12.9 Wallet Summary
Manage Wallet / My Wallet shall show at minimum:
- total issued coins,
- total utilized coins,
- available / unused coins,
- and wallet transaction history including debit reasons where applicable.

### 12.10 Issuance Ledger
Every coin assignment shall create a ledger transaction record with metadata such as:
- issuer,
- tenant,
- target type,
- target entity,
- quantity,
- timestamp,
- and reason / note where applicable.

### 12.11 Utilization Tracking
The system shall separately track utilization events so that issued and used totals are not conflated.

### 12.12 Balance Calculation
Available coin balance shall be calculated as:

**Available Coins = Total Issued Coins - Total Utilized Coins - Other Valid Debits + Valid Credits**

For MVP, the primary calculation may be simplified to:

**Available Coins = Total Issued Coins - Total Utilized Coins**

### 12.13 Auditability
The system shall preserve auditable wallet transactions and shall not depend only on a mutable current balance field.

### 12.14 Role-Based Access
Only the SuperAdmin shall be able to issue coins in this version of the Epic.

---

## 13. User Experience Requirements

### 13.1 SuperAdmin Flow
The expected SuperAdmin flow is:

1. Open SuperAdmin dashboard.
2. View wallet summary tile showing utilized / issued count.
3. Click tile or open **Manage Coins** from navigation.
4. Select tenant.
5. Select target user type.
6. Select specific target entity.
7. Enter coin quantity.
8. Submit assignment.
9. View confirmation and updated ledger / summary.

### 13.2 User Wallet Flow
The expected recipient flow is:

1. Log in to StudioVerse.
2. Open **My Wallet**.
3. See current available coins.
4. Review issued and utilized values.
5. Review recent wallet history where applicable.

---

## 14. Data Requirements

### 14.1 Wallet Entity
The Wallet entity shall support at minimum:

- `id`
- `tenantId`
- `ownerType`
- `ownerId`
- `ownerDisplayName`
- `totalIssuedCoins`
- `totalUtilizedCoins`
- `availableCoins`
- `status`
- `createdAt`
- `updatedAt`

### 14.2 Wallet Transaction Entity
The Wallet Transaction entity shall support at minimum:

- `id`
- `walletId`
- `tenantId`
- `transactionType`
- `creditOrDebit`
- `coinAmount`
- `issuedBy`
- `issuedByRole`
- `targetType`
- `targetEntityId`
- `targetEntityName`
- `reason`
- `referenceType`
- `referenceId`
- `balanceBefore`
- `balanceAfter`
- `status`
- `createdAt`

### 14.3 Dashboard Summary Model
The dashboard summary may be derived through aggregation and shall support:

- `totalIssuedCoins`
- `totalUtilizedCoins`
- `totalUnusedCoins`
- `tenantCountCovered`
- `updatedAt`

### 14.4 Suggested Transaction Types
The system should support transaction types such as:

- `issue`
- `adjustment_credit`
- `adjustment_debit`
- `utilization`
- `reversal`
- `expiry`

Even if only `issue` and `utilization` are active in the first version, the schema should be future-ready.

---

## 15. Business Rules

- Only SuperAdmin may assign coins in this release.
- Coin assignment must be tenant-aware.
- A target recipient must belong to the selected tenant.
- Coin quantity must be a positive whole number.
- Coin assignment must create a ledger transaction.
- My Wallet must reflect valid issued balance for the logged-in owner.
- Utilized coins must be tracked separately from issued coins.
- Unused coins must equal issued coins minus utilized coins, unless additional debit rules are later introduced.
- Dashboard summary must aggregate totals across all tenants.
- Clicking the wallet summary tile must open the Manage Coins page.
- The system must preserve auditability and should not rely only on overwriting one balance field.

---

## 16. Architectural Recommendation

The wallet system should be implemented using a **ledger-first model** rather than a balance-only model.

That means:
- every allocation or usage event creates a transaction record,
- balances may be stored as denormalized summary fields for performance,
- but the source of truth remains the transaction history.

This is recommended because it improves:
- auditability,
- debugging,
- reconciliation,
- future reporting,
- and extensibility for coin-based platform usage.

The platform should maintain:
- a wallet entity for current summarized balance,
- and a wallet transaction collection for immutable balance events.

---

## 17. Dashboard and Reporting Model

The SuperAdmin dashboard must present a compact but meaningful summary of wallet state through a tile such as:

**Utilized / Issued = 10 / 20**

Interpretation:
- **10** = total utilized coins
- **20** = total issued coins across all tenants

The system should also be capable of deriving:
- unused coins,
- utilization rate,
- and allocation coverage by tenant,
even if only the utilized/issued tile is shown in the first release.

---

## 18. User Stories

### US-E5-01 — SuperAdmin Can Access Manage Coins
**As a** SuperAdmin,  
**I want** a Manage Coins menu item,  
**so that** I can administer platform coin allocations.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Manage Coins appears in SuperAdmin navigation.
- Selecting it opens the coin management page.

---

### US-E5-02 — SuperAdmin Can Assign Coins by Tenant
**As a** SuperAdmin,  
**I want** to first select a tenant,  
**so that** coin allocations remain tenant-aware.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Tenant selection is required before target lookup.
- Only valid tenant-linked targets are shown afterward.

---

### US-E5-03 — SuperAdmin Can Assign Coins by Target Type
**As a** SuperAdmin,  
**I want** to choose whether I am assigning coins to a Company, Coach / Professional, or Individual,  
**so that** allocations are directed correctly.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- User type options are visible after tenant selection.
- Target lookup changes based on selected user type.

---

### US-E5-04 — SuperAdmin Can Issue Coins
**As a** SuperAdmin,  
**I want** to assign a selected number of coins to a valid target,  
**so that** wallet balances are controlled centrally.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Quantity must be a positive integer.
- Issuance creates a wallet transaction.
- Wallet summary updates after successful issue.

---

### US-E5-05 — User Can View Wallet Balance
**As a** logged-in user,  
**I want** to see my wallet balance in My Wallet,  
**so that** I know how many coins are available to me.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- My Wallet is visible to the logged-in owner.
- Available balance, issued balance, and utilized balance are shown.

---

### US-E5-06 — Dashboard Shows Issued vs Utilized Coins
**As a** SuperAdmin,  
**I want** the dashboard to show total utilized and total issued coins,  
**so that** I can quickly assess platform-level wallet usage.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Dashboard tile shows utilized / issued format.
- Values aggregate across all tenants.

---

### US-E5-07 — Dashboard Tile Opens Manage Coins
**As a** SuperAdmin,  
**I want** to click the wallet summary tile and go directly to Manage Coins,  
**so that** I can quickly move from insight to action.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Tile is clickable.
- Clicking opens Manage Coins page.

---

### US-E5-08 — Professional Can Request Coins from Associated Company (Completed)
**As a** Professional,  
**I want** to submit a coin request to my associated Company,  
**so that** I can request additional coins for platform usage without requiring SuperAdmin coordination.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Request Coins page is accessible at `/<tenant>/request-coins`.
- Professional can enter a quantity and submit a request.
- Request is stored in Firestore with status "pending" and includes Professional ID and associated Company ID.
- Firestore rules allow Professional create if requesterProfessionalId matches auth.uid and status is "pending".
- Request is routed to the correct Company based on Professional.associatedCompanyId from profile context.

---

### US-E5-09 — Company Can Approve or Deny Coin Requests with Ruleset Enforcement (Completed)
**As a** Company user,  
**I want** to view, approve, and deny pending coin requests from associated Professionals,  
**so that** I can govern coin distribution to team members responsibly.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Manage Wallet page shows pending coin request count as a live badge.
- Company users can click to open coin requests modal.
- Modal displays all pending requests for the Company.
- Approve action transitions request status to "approved" and adds coins to Professional's wallet via ledger transaction.
- Deny action transitions request status to "denied" with optional reason field.
- Firestore rules restrict approve/deny to Company users only (isCompanyUser() constraint).
- Badge count refreshes in real-time after action is taken.
- Delete operations are permanently disabled (soft-delete pattern only).

---

## 19. QA Considerations

QA should validate the following scenarios:

- SuperAdmin can see Manage Coins navigation.
- Dashboard tile displays correct utilized and issued totals.
- Clicking the tile opens Manage Coins.
- Tenant selection filters available target entities correctly.
- Company-level, Coach-level, and Individual-level assignment flows work correctly.
- Invalid tenant-target combinations are blocked.
- Coin quantity validation prevents zero, negative, or invalid values.
- Issuance creates a ledger transaction.
- Wallet summaries update after issuance.
- My Wallet shows correct values for the logged-in owner.
- Utilized and unused calculations are correct.
- Cross-tenant aggregation works correctly for dashboard totals.
- Unauthorized roles cannot issue coins.

**Coin Request Lifecycle (US-E5-08/09) Testing:**
- Professional can access `/<tenant>/request-coins` page.
- Professional can submit a coin request with valid quantity.
- Firestore rules enforce: requests can only be created by the requesting Professional (auth.uid matches requesterProfessionalId).
- Firestore rules enforce: requests with invalid status or zero amount are rejected.
- Professional requests are routed to the correct Company based on profile association.
- Company users see pending coin request count on Manage Wallet page badge.
- Badge count is accurate and updates after approval/denial.
- Company users can open modal and view all pending requests for their company.
- Company users can approve a request (status transitions to "approved", coins added to Professional's wallet).
- Company users can deny a request (status transitions to "denied").
- Firestore rules restrict approve/deny operations to Company users only.
- Unauthorized roles (Individual, Professional without company role) cannot approve/deny requests.
- Hard delete is disabled for coin requests (only soft-delete via status updates permitted).
- Coin request transactions appear in Professional wallet history after approval.
- Coin request transactions appear in Company audit/transaction history.

---

## 20. Integration Considerations

This Epic is expected to integrate with:

- authentication and role-based access control,
- tenant and user directory services,
- dashboard summary widgets,
- future controlled consumption flows,
- analytics and reporting services,
- and possible future billing or entitlement layers.

Wallet issuance must work cleanly with StudioVerse’s tenant-aware architecture and role model.

---

## 21. Dependencies

This Epic depends on:
- authenticated platform access,
- tenant-aware authorization,
- stable user and tenant identity records,
- dashboard framework,
- and backend persistence for wallet and transaction records.

This Epic may later be extended by:
- Wallet Utilization Epic,
- Wallet Expiry Rules Epic,
- Wallet Purchase / Billing Epic,
- Wallet Analytics and Reconciliation Epic,
- and Wallet Adjustments / Reversal Epic.

---

## 22. Risks and Considerations

- If the system stores only a mutable balance and no transaction ledger, auditability will be weak.
- If tenant scoping is not enforced, coins may be assigned to the wrong entity.
- If utilized and issued values are not separated, dashboard reporting will be misleading.
- If My Wallet does not clearly distinguish available vs utilized, users may misunderstand their balance.
- If company-, coach-, and individual-level ownership rules are not modeled carefully, later consumption logic may become difficult.

---

## 23. Definition of Done

This Epic is considered complete when:

- Manage Coins is available in SuperAdmin navigation.
- SuperAdmin can select tenant, target type, and target entity.
- SuperAdmin can assign coins successfully.
- Coin assignment creates transaction ledger records.
- Logged-in recipients can see their balance in My Wallet.
- Dashboard tile shows utilized and issued totals correctly.
- Clicking the tile opens Manage Coins.
- Product, Engineering, and QA approve workflow and data correctness.

---

## 24. Copilot Implementation Note

This document may be used as an implementation specification for GitHub Copilot or other AI coding tools.

When using this document as a coding reference:
- treat this file as the source of truth for Wallet and Coin terminology,
- implement coin issuance using a ledger-first model,
- separate issued, utilized, and available values,
- keep all assignment flows tenant-aware,
- restrict coin issuance to SuperAdmin in the first version,
- and ensure dashboard summaries are derived from valid wallet data.

---

## 25. Document End State

This PRD is the source document for:
- stakeholder circulation,
- backlog refinement,
- engineering breakdown,
- QA test design,
- dashboard widget planning,
- and Copilot-assisted implementation guidance.

---

# StudioVerse
## Product Requirements Document
## EPIC E6 — Manage Profile and Profile Completion

**Document Type:** Functional Product Requirements Document

- Mandatory profile completion is evaluated and surfaced on the dashboard.
- Assignment readiness gating is active and currently blocks assignment/self-registration flows until required profile fields are complete where applicable.

> Do not invent alternate terminology.  
> Use "Profile", "Update Profile", "Profile Completion", and "My Profile" consistently.  
> Keep the system role-aware, tenant-aware, and future-ready for coaching, training, and recruitment use cases.

---

## 1. Purpose

This document defines the business and functional requirements for the **Manage Profile** capability in StudioVerse.

The purpose of this Epic is to create a role-aware profile system that captures the minimum required information needed for users to participate meaningfully in the platform while allowing a stepped approach to profile enrichment over time.

This Epic also establishes the profile-completion gating behavior required before an Individual can be assigned an Assessment, Program, or Event, and lays the foundation for richer assessment quality, recruitment usage, search, matching, and future public profile scenarios such as **Search Coach**.

---

## 2. Audience

### Business Stakeholders
This document explains how Profile becomes a core platform object that supports onboarding, assignment readiness, better assessment quality, professional discovery, and future recruitment value creation.

### Product Owners / Product Management
This document defines profile scope, mandatory versus optional fields, role-specific profile sections, completion gating, dashboard prompting, and long-term extensibility for public and professional use cases.

### Engineering Teams
This document provides the functional requirements needed to implement profile schemas, section-based update flows, completion-status evaluation, assignment eligibility checks, and role-dependent UI rendering.

### QA Teams
This document provides the basis for validating profile completion, mandatory field enforcement, dashboard nudges, assignment gating, role-specific forms, editability rules, and future profile extensibility.

### Design Teams
This document describes the expected experience for profile completion prompts, profile update pages, section-based forms, and informational guidance encouraging users to enrich optional data.

---

## 3. Executive Summary

StudioVerse needs a structured Profile capability that supports the practical reality that users cannot be expected to complete a very large form all at once.

Therefore, the platform will follow a **stepped profile completion approach**. On first access to the dashboard, users who have not completed the mandatory part of their profile will be shown a top-level prompt asking them to complete their profile.

Selecting that prompt will take the user to an **Update Profile** page that contains a full profile layout with multiple sections. The mandatory section must be completed before an Individual becomes eligible to receive platform assignments such as Assessments, Programs, or Events.

All remaining sections may remain optional in the early stage, but the product will explicitly guide the user by stating that richer profile information can lead to **better assessments** and stronger platform outcomes.

The Profile object will support multiple role types:
- **Individual**
- **Professional**
- **Company**

Professionals will have additional professional-facing fields, and in a future Epic such as **Search Coach**, that information can power a world-visible public coach profile.

Companies will also have their own basic profile structure including company identity and location data.

---

## 4. Business Context

StudioVerse is a multi-tenant platform spanning coaching, training, and recruitment-oriented scenarios, which makes Profile more than a simple personal information form.

Profile data can support:
- onboarding readiness,
- assignment eligibility,
- better contextual assessments,
- professional and recruitment referrals,
- future search and discovery,
- tenant-aware data completeness,
- and better decision-making across the platform.

Because the platform includes Individuals, Professionals, and Company entities, a one-size-fits-all profile form would be too rigid and too heavy. A role-aware and phased model is therefore required.

---

## 5. Problem Statement

StudioVerse currently requires a formal Profile capability that:
- captures the minimum mandatory data needed for platform participation,
- supports role-aware extensions,
- and avoids forcing users to fill everything in one go.

The platform also needs a clear mechanism to ensure that assignment-related experiences do not begin before the Individual has provided the minimum necessary profile information.

Without this capability:
- users may enter core journeys with incomplete identity data,
- assessments may lack useful context,
- assignments may fail operationally,
- profile quality will vary significantly,
- and the platform will not have a reliable foundation for future search, recruitment, and public profile use cases.

---

## 6. Objectives

The objectives of this Epic are:

- Establish Profile as a canonical role-aware platform object.
- Support profile creation and update for Individuals, Professionals, and Companies.
- Define mandatory versus optional fields clearly.
- Support a stepped profile completion experience instead of forcing full completion upfront.
- Show a dashboard prompt requesting profile completion when mandatory data is missing.
- Route users from the dashboard prompt to an **Update Profile** page.
- Gate assignment eligibility for Individuals until mandatory profile fields are complete.
- Support optional profile enrichment through structured sections.
- Encourage optional enrichment by communicating that richer profiles lead to better assessments.
- Support optional profile photo upload.
- Lay groundwork for future public Professional profile visibility in Search Coach.

---

## 7. Non-Objectives

The following are out of scope for this Epic:

- full public profile pages for search engines,
- advanced social networking functionality,
- recommendations engine logic,
- professional ranking algorithms,
- resume parsing,
- external verification workflows,
- public coach search listings,
- and recruitment application pipelines.

These may be built in later epics, but this Epic focuses on profile capture, completion, gating, and update flows.

---

## 8. In-Scope Capabilities

This Epic includes the following capabilities:

- role-aware profile model,
- mandatory and optional profile fields,
- dashboard reminder / banner for incomplete mandatory profile,
- Update Profile page with section-based layout,
- optional profile picture upload,
- assignment readiness gating for Individuals,
- profile completion status evaluation,
- Professional-specific fields,
- Company-specific fields,
- and future-ready schema support for public profile use cases.

---

## 9. Out-of-Scope Capabilities

This Epic does not include:

- Search Coach public listing pages,
- resume recommendation engines,
- AI-written profile summaries,
- public shareable profile URLs,
- endorsements and ratings,
- and company verification workflows.

---

## 10. Domain Definitions

### 10.1 Profile
A Profile is the structured identity and contextual information object associated with a platform actor such as an Individual, Professional, or Company.

### 10.2 Mandatory Profile Fields
Mandatory Profile Fields are the minimum set of fields that must be completed in order for the user to be considered profile-complete for core participation.

### 10.3 Optional Profile Fields
Optional Profile Fields are enrichment fields that are not required to activate basic eligibility but improve future platform outcomes.

### 10.4 Profile Completion
Profile Completion is the state in which all mandatory fields for the relevant role have been provided and validated.

### 10.5 Profile Enrichment
Profile Enrichment refers to the completion of additional non-mandatory sections that improve context, assessment quality, searchability, and future matching.

### 10.6 Update Profile Page
The Update Profile Page is the structured screen where the user reviews and completes profile information section by section.

### 10.7 Assignment Eligibility Gate
The Assignment Eligibility Gate is the rule that prevents assignment of certain experiences, such as Assessments, Programs, or Events, until an Individual’s mandatory profile is complete.

---

## 11. User Roles

### 11.1 Individual
The Individual must complete mandatory personal profile fields before being eligible for Assessments, Programs, or Events.

### 11.2 Professional
The Professional must also maintain mandatory identity information and may additionally complete professional-specific fields that will later support public profile visibility and search.

### 11.3 Company
The Company profile must capture basic organization information and required location or identity data needed for platform usage.

### 11.4 SuperAdmin
The SuperAdmin may view profile completeness indirectly through downstream modules and can rely on profile readiness rules for assignment workflows.

---

## 12. Profile Strategy

### 12.1 Stepped Completion Approach
The platform shall not require every possible profile field to be completed at once.

Instead, the platform shall:
- require completion of a mandatory profile section first,
- allow all other sections to remain optional,
- and encourage users to enrich their profile progressively over time.

### 12.2 Dashboard Reminder
If a logged-in user has not completed the mandatory profile section, the dashboard shall show a visible top-level message asking the user to complete their profile.

Example intent:
- “Please complete your profile to continue.”
- “Complete your mandatory profile details before assignments can be enabled.”

### 12.3 Update Profile Redirection
Selecting the dashboard reminder shall take the user to the **Update Profile** page.

### 12.4 Enrichment Messaging
The Update Profile page shall communicate that while some fields are optional, completing them can improve platform outcomes such as better assessments.

---

## 13. Profile Types

### 13.1 Individual Profile
The Individual Profile shall include personal identity, contact, location, education, experience, and enrichment fields relevant to assessments and future recruitment support.

### 13.2 Professional Profile
The Professional Profile shall include all relevant mandatory identity fields plus professional-facing fields such as expertise, experience, professional summary, and future public-discovery-supporting fields.

### 13.3 Company Profile
The Company Profile shall include basic company information, address and location details, and other foundational organization fields needed for tenant-level identity.

---

## 14. Field Requirements

### 14.1 Base Fields Proposed by Stakeholder Direction
The following base needs have been identified:

- Name — mandatory, not editable.
- User Type — mandatory, not editable.
- Name of Company — optional.
- Address — optional.
- City — mandatory.
- Phone — mandatory, not editable.
- Email Address — mandatory, not editable.
- Highest Degree Held — optional.
- Years of Experience — optional.
- Profile Picture — optional.

### 14.2 Recommended Individual Mandatory Fields
For Individuals, the mandatory section shall include at minimum:

- Full Name
- User Type
- City
- Phone Number
- Email Address

### 14.3 Recommended Individual Optional Fields
For Individuals, optional fields should include at minimum:

- Profile Picture
- Company Name
- Address
- State / Province
- Country
- Highest Degree Held
- Field of Study
- Years of Experience
- Current Role / Designation
- Skills
- Short Bio / Summary
- LinkedIn URL

### 14.4 Professional Mandatory Fields
For Professionals, mandatory fields should include at minimum:

- Full Name
- User Type
- City
- Phone Number
- Email Address

### 14.5 Professional Optional / Enrichment Fields
For Professionals, optional enrichment fields should include:

- Profile Picture
- Professional Headline
- Company Name
- Expertise Areas
- Highest Degree Held
- Certifications
- Years of Experience
- Coaching / Training Experience
- Industry Focus
- Short Professional Bio
- Languages Spoken
- LinkedIn URL
- Website / Portfolio URL

These fields are important because they create the basis for a later public professional profile in **Search Coach**.

### 14.6 Company Mandatory Fields
For Company profiles, mandatory fields should include at minimum:

- Company Name
- Company Type / Tenant Type
- City
- Primary Phone Number
- Primary Email Address

### 14.7 Company Optional Fields
For Company profiles, optional fields should include:

- Logo
- Address
- State / Province
- Country
- Pincode / Postal Code
- Website URL
- Company Description
- Industry
- Number of Employees
- Contact Person Name

---

## 15. Editability Rules

The following fields shall be non-editable for user-managed updates where they are identity-controlled by the platform:

- Name
- User Type
- Phone Number
- Email Address

Other fields may be editable, subject to profile-type rules and future admin governance.

The platform should clearly distinguish:
- system-controlled identity fields,
- editable profile fields,
- and optional enrichment fields.

---

## 16. Profile Completion Rules

### 16.1 Completion Logic
A profile shall be considered complete when all mandatory fields for that role are present and valid.

### 16.2 Individual Assignment Gate
An Individual profile must be complete before the platform allows assignment of:
- Assessments,
- Programs,
- Events.

### 16.3 Optional Fields
Optional fields shall not block basic eligibility.

### 16.4 Sectioned Completion
The platform may show section-level completion status, but the key gating rule shall be based on completion of the mandatory section.

---

## 17. User Experience Requirements

### 17.1 Dashboard Prompt
When the logged-in user has not completed mandatory profile information, the dashboard shall show a prominent prompt at the top of the dashboard requesting completion.

### 17.2 Update Profile Page
The Update Profile page shall be a full structured page with multiple sections rather than a small inline edit form.

The page shall include:
- mandatory section,
- optional enrichment sections,
- profile picture upload area,
- and explanatory messaging.

### 17.3 Guidance Messaging
The Update Profile page shall show guidance explaining that optional fields are not required but can improve platform outcomes, especially assessments.

### 17.4 Save Behavior
The system shall allow users to save the profile progressively, even if optional sections remain incomplete.

### 17.5 Visual Completion Feedback
The system should show whether the mandatory section is complete and whether the user is eligible for assignments.

---

## 18. Data Requirements

### 18.1 Base Profile Entity
The Profile entity shall support at minimum:

- `id`
- `userId`
- `tenantId`
- `profileType`
- `fullName`
- `userType`
- `companyName`
- `addressLine1`
- `addressLine2`
- `city`
- `state`
- `country`
- `postalCode`
- `phone`
- `email`
- `profilePhotoUrl`
- `highestDegreeHeld`
- `fieldOfStudy`
- `yearsOfExperience`
- `bio`
- `skills`
- `linkedinUrl`
- `websiteUrl`
- `mandatoryProfileCompleted`
- `profileCompletionPercent`
- `assignmentEligible`
- `status`
- `createdAt`
- `updatedAt`

### 18.2 Professional Extension Fields
Where the profile type is Professional, the schema should additionally support:

- `professionalHeadline`
- `expertiseAreas`
- `certifications`
- `coachingExperienceYears`
- `trainingExperienceYears`
- `industryFocus`
- `languagesSpoken`
- `publicProfileReady`

### 18.3 Company Profile Fields
Where the profile type is Company, the schema should additionally support:

- `companyLegalName`
- `companyDisplayName`
- `companyType`
- `companyLogoUrl`
- `companyDescription`
- `industry`
- `employeeCountRange`
- `primaryContactName`

### 18.4 System Evaluation Fields
The system shall support fields or computed values indicating:

- whether mandatory data is complete,
- whether the profile is eligible for assignment,
- and what percent of all profile fields have been completed.

---

## 19. Business Rules

- The platform shall support a stepped profile completion approach.
- Mandatory fields must be completed before Individuals can receive assignments.
- Optional fields must not block core onboarding.
- Name, User Type, Phone, and Email shall be treated as non-editable identity fields in user-managed profile updates.
- Profile Picture shall be optional.
- Professional profiles shall support additional professional context fields.
- Company profiles shall support company-specific basic information and location fields.
- The dashboard reminder shall be shown when mandatory completion is incomplete.
- The Update Profile page shall include both mandatory and optional sections.
- The system shall explicitly encourage optional enrichment by communicating its value for better assessments.

---

## 20. Architectural Recommendation

The Profile system should be implemented as a role-aware but unified profile model.

That means:
- one canonical profile object,
- shared base fields across all roles,
- role-specific extension fields,
- and computed completion-state fields used by the dashboard and assignment workflows.

This approach is preferable to creating unrelated profile models for each role because it improves:
- consistency,
- validation reuse,
- analytics,
- extensibility,
- and downstream feature integration.

---

## 21. Integration Considerations

This Epic is expected to integrate with:

- authentication and identity,
- dashboard prompting,
- assessments,
- programs,
- events,
- future recruitment workflows,
- and future Search Coach functionality.

Profile readiness must be available to assignment-related modules so that they can prevent assignment when mandatory profile completion is missing.

---

## 22. User Stories

### US-E6-01 — User Sees Dashboard Prompt for Incomplete Profile
**As a** logged-in user,  
**I want** to see a clear dashboard prompt when my mandatory profile is incomplete,  
**so that** I know I must complete it before continuing.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Prompt appears when mandatory fields are incomplete.
- Prompt is shown prominently at the top of the dashboard.
- Prompt links to Update Profile page.

---

### US-E6-02 — User Can Open Update Profile Page
**As a** logged-in user,  
**I want** a full Update Profile page with structured sections,  
**so that** I can complete my profile in a guided way.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Update Profile is a full page.
- Mandatory and optional sections are clearly separated.
- User can save progressive updates.

---

### US-E6-03 — Individual Must Complete Mandatory Profile Before Assignment
**As a** Product Owner,  
**I want** Individuals to complete mandatory profile fields before assignment,  
**so that** the platform has enough context before assigning experiences.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Individuals with incomplete mandatory fields are not assignment eligible.
- Assessments, Programs, and Events cannot be assigned until profile is complete.

---

### US-E6-04 — Optional Fields Improve Profile Quality Without Blocking Usage
**As a** user,  
**I want** optional profile fields to remain non-blocking,  
**so that** I can get started without completing everything at once.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Optional sections do not block mandatory completion.
- System messaging explains value of enrichment.

---

### US-E6-05 — Professional Can Maintain a Professional-Oriented Profile
**As a** Professional,  
**I want** extra professional fields in my profile,  
**so that** the platform can later use them for discovery and profile visibility.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Professional profile displays additional relevant fields.
- Data is stored in role-aware structure.
- Future public profile support is not blocked.

---

### US-E6-06 — Company Can Maintain Basic Company Profile
**As a** Company,  
**I want** a basic profile with company information and location,  
**so that** the tenant identity is correctly represented in the platform.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Company profile has company-specific fields.
- Mandatory company data can be completed and stored.

---

## 23. QA Considerations

QA should validate the following scenarios:

- dashboard reminder appears correctly for incomplete profile,
- dashboard reminder does not appear for completed mandatory profile,
- clicking reminder opens Update Profile page,
- mandatory fields are enforced by role,
- non-editable fields remain locked,
- optional fields remain editable where appropriate,
- profile picture upload works where enabled,
- progressive save works,
- completion status is calculated correctly,
- assignment eligibility changes correctly when mandatory fields become complete,
- Individuals with incomplete profile cannot be assigned Assessments, Programs, or Events,
- Professional-specific fields appear only for Professionals where applicable,
- Company-specific fields appear only for Company profiles where applicable.

---

## 24. Risks and Considerations

- If too many fields are made mandatory initially, onboarding friction will increase.
- If mandatory fields are too few, downstream modules may lack sufficient context.
- If role-specific profile fields are not modeled early, future public and recruitment use cases may require rework.
- If editability rules are unclear, identity data quality may degrade.
- If the dashboard prompt is weak or easy to ignore, profile completion rates may remain low.

---

## 25. Definition of Done

This Epic is considered complete when:

- Profile supports Individuals, Professionals, and Companies.
- Mandatory and optional fields are clearly modeled.
- Dashboard prompt appears when mandatory profile is incomplete.
- Update Profile page supports section-based completion.
- Optional profile picture upload is supported.
- Identity-controlled fields are non-editable.
- Individual assignment gating works for Assessments, Programs, and Events.
- Professional-specific and Company-specific profile sections work correctly.
- Product, Engineering, and QA approve profile flow and data behavior.

---

## 26. Copilot Implementation Note

This document may be used as an implementation specification for GitHub Copilot or other AI coding tools.

When using this document as a coding reference:
- treat this file as the source of truth for Profile terminology and behavior,
- implement a stepped profile completion model,
- separate mandatory and optional sections clearly,
- keep identity-controlled fields non-editable,
- gate Individual assignments until mandatory profile completion is true,
- support role-aware sections for Individual, Professional, and Company,
- and keep the model future-ready for Search Coach and recruitment use cases.

---

## 27. Document End State

This PRD is the source document for:
- stakeholder circulation,
- backlog refinement,
- engineering breakdown,
- QA test design,
- onboarding flow planning,
- and Copilot-assisted implementation guidance.

---

**End of Document**

# StudioVerse
## Product Requirements Document
## EPIC E7 — Activity Assignment, Recommendation, and Self-Registration

**Document Type:** Functional Product Requirements Document  
**Epic ID:** E7  
**Version:** 1.1  
**Date:** April 12, 2026  
**Product:** StudioVerse  
**Primary Module:** Activity Assignment  
**Primary Capability:** Assign, recommend, and self-register into activities  
**Primary Actors:** Company, Professional, Individual Assignee, Logged-in Self User, SuperAdmin  
**Audience:** Business Stakeholders, Product Owners, Engineering, QA, Design, Architecture, Wallet / Profile / Activity Teams  
**Status:** Draft

> Implementation instruction:  
> Read this file as the primary source of truth for the Activity Assignment functionality.  
> Do not invent alternate terminology.  
> Use "Assign", "Recommend", "Register Now", "Try Now", "Assigned Activity", "Assignee", and "My activities" consistently.  
> Keep the system tenant-aware, wallet-aware, and reusable across Programs, Assessments, and Events.

## Implementation status snapshot — April 12, 2026

- Assign, recommend, Register Now, and Try Now flows are implemented across Programs, Assessments, and Events.
- Search-first assignment now uses staged states: search, found, not-found, and confirm.
- For not-found users, the flow collects first name, last name, and the missing contact field so phone and email are both captured before completion.
- Unknown assignees are auto-provisioned as Individuals and receive a zero-balance wallet if a wallet does not yet exist.
- Assign-to-other debits the assignor-owned wallet. Self-assignment debits the logged-in user wallet only when credits are greater than zero. Zero-credit self-assignment bypasses wallet validation.
- Recommendations are persisted as activity records with status `recommended` so they appear in My activities.
- My activities and Manage Wallet pages are now part of the implemented user journey.

---

## 1. Purpose

This document defines the business and functional requirements for the **Activity Assignment** capability in StudioVerse.

The purpose of this Epic is to allow eligible logged-in users such as **Company** and **Professional** actors to assign or recommend activities to another person using a lightweight search-first workflow based on phone number or email address.

This Epic also supports a self-directed activity journey where a logged-in user can click **Register Now** or **Try Now** and assign the activity to himself or herself without entering search details.

This Epic establishes the assignment confirmation rules, wallet validation and coin deduction behavior, assignee lookup flow, self-assignment flow, assigned activity records, and post-login visibility of assigned activities for the recipient.

---

## 2. Audience

### Business Stakeholders
This document explains how StudioVerse can operationalize Programs, Assessments, and Events by allowing platform actors to direct those activities to other people or to themselves in a controlled, trackable, wallet-aware way.

### Product Owners / Product Management
This document defines the workflow, eligibility logic, wallet validation, assignment record creation, recipient discovery, self-registration behavior, and assignment visibility rules for assigned activities.

### Engineering Teams
This document provides the functional requirements needed to implement assignment popups, user lookup by email or phone, validation, self-assignment behavior, wallet checks, transaction creation, and assignee activity visibility.

### QA Teams
This document provides the basis for validating search behavior, user lookup, self-assignment, assignment gating, insufficient wallet messaging, wallet deductions, activity creation, and assigned activity display behavior.

### Design Teams
This document describes the modal-based assignment interaction, self-assignment action flow, conditional states, messaging, and user feedback needed to make assignment and recommendation flows intuitive and reliable.

---

## 3. Executive Summary

Once logged in, a **Company** or **Professional** should be able to assign or recommend a Program, Assessment, or Event to someone else from within the product.

When the user clicks an **Assign** or **Recommend** action, the platform shall open a popup that asks for either a **phone number** or an **email address**. The user may provide either one, and the system must validate the input before performing search.

If the assignee already exists in registered users, the system shall display the user’s **phone number, email address, first name, and last name** and enable the next action button such as **Assign** or **Recommend**.

If the user is not found, the platform shall inform the assigner that the user was not found and shall request **first name** and **last name**, so that assignment can still proceed in a controlled way as a newly identified or not-yet-registered recipient.

In addition, when a logged-in user clicks **Register Now** or **Try Now** on a supported activity, the system shall follow the same assignment flow but assign the activity to the same logged-in user. In this self-assignment flow, the system shall not ask for phone number, email address, first name, or last name because the assignee is already known.

Assignment can only happen if the wallet responsible for funding the assignment has enough coins for the activity credit requirement. If insufficient coins are available, the platform must show an error message such as **“Not enough coins, please get more coins.”** If enough coins are available, the platform shall show a confirmation that **n coins will be reduced** for the assignment. For zero-credit activities, assignment may proceed without wallet blocking. Once assignment or recommendation is completed, the corresponding activity record shall be created and the recipient shall later see the activity in **My activities** after login.

---

## 4. Business Context

StudioVerse is designed as a structured multi-tenant platform where Programs, Assessments, and Events are not only consumed directly, but can also be assigned, recommended, or self-started by platform actors.

This means the platform needs a lightweight but reliable mechanism to:
- find a target user,
- confirm whether they are already registered,
- support controlled assignment,
- support self-assignment,
- debit the appropriate wallet,
- and make that activity visible to the assignee in their own experience.

Because the platform already uses wallet-based coin allocation for entitlement, assignment must also integrate directly with coin availability and wallet transaction tracking.

---

## 5. Problem Statement

StudioVerse currently needs a formal Activity Assignment capability that allows a Company or Professional to assign or recommend an activity to another user without navigating away into a long workflow.

The platform also needs to support a self-serve model in which a logged-in user can directly click **Register Now** or **Try Now** and receive the activity as his or her own assigned activity.

The platform also needs to handle both known and unknown assignees. Some recipients may already exist as registered users, while others may need to be identified through minimal information at the time of assignment.

Without this capability:
- activities remain isolated rather than operationally shareable,
- companies and professionals cannot direct users into the right journey,
- users cannot self-initiate supported activities consistently,
- wallet debits cannot be tied to assignment events,
- and recipients cannot see a reliable list of activities assigned to them after login.

---

## 6. Objectives

The objectives of this Epic are:

- Allow Company and Professional users to assign or recommend activities to others after login.
- Support assignment and recommendation flows from Programs, Assessments, and Events.
- Support self-assignment through **Register Now** and **Try Now** for supported activity types.
- Open a popup-based flow when the Assign / Recommend action is clicked.
- Allow assignee search by **phone number** or **email address** for assignment to others.
- Validate search input before lookup.
- Display first and last name when a registered user is found.
- Allow not-found users to be identified through first name, last name, and whichever contact field (phone or email) was not part of the original search.
- Create an **Assigned Activity** record upon successful assignment.
- Enforce wallet balance validation before assignment.
- Show insufficient wallet messaging when coins are not enough.
- Show confirmation before deducting coins.
- Record the wallet transaction linked to the assignment.
- Show assigned activities to the assignee after login.
- Provide a wallet transaction view link from **My Wallet**.

---

## 7. Non-Objectives

The following are out of scope for this Epic:

- external invitations through SMS or email delivery,
- unregistered-user onboarding workflow,
- public recommendation feeds,
- group or bulk assignment,
- advanced assignment analytics,
- cancellation and refund flows,
- and recommender ranking logic.

This Epic focuses on the core assignment / recommendation / self-registration transaction and visibility flow.

---

## 8. In-Scope Capabilities

This Epic includes the following capabilities:

- Assign / Recommend button on supported activity types.
- Register Now / Try Now button on supported activity types.
- Popup-based assignee search for assignment to others.
- Search by email or phone.
- Input validation.
- Registered-user lookup.
- Not-found state with first name / last name collection.
- Self-assignment without search inputs.
- Wallet coin sufficiency check.
- Confirmation prompt before wallet debit.
- Assigned Activity record creation.
- Wallet transaction creation.
- Assigned activity visibility to assignee.
- Wallet transaction visibility through My Wallet.

---

## 9. Out-of-Scope Capabilities

This Epic does not include:

- third-party messaging integrations,
- assignment revocation with coin reversal,
- bulk assignment import,
- assignment reminder automation,
- and multi-step onboarding for new external recipients.

---

## 10. Domain Definitions

### 10.1 Activity
An Activity is a platform object that may be assigned, recommended, or self-registered by a user, such as an Assessment, Program, or Event.

### 10.2 Assigned Activity
An Assigned Activity is the record created when one user directs an activity to another user or to himself / herself through an assignment, recommendation, or self-registration action.

### 10.3 Assigner
The Assigner is the logged-in Company or Professional user who initiates the assignment or recommendation.

### 10.4 Assignee
The Assignee is the target user to whom the activity is assigned or recommended.

### 10.5 Self-Assignment
Self-Assignment is the flow in which a logged-in user clicks **Register Now** or **Try Now** and the activity is assigned to the same user.

### 10.6 Recommendation
A Recommendation is a lighter form of directed activity suggestion. In UI terms it may share much of the same flow as assignment, though the downstream status may differ.

### 10.7 Activity Credit Requirement
The Activity Credit Requirement is the number of coins required to complete the assignment of a given activity.

### 10.8 Wallet Transaction
A Wallet Transaction is the ledger record created when coins are debited due to a successful assignment.

---

## 11. User Roles

### 11.1 Company
A Company user may assign or recommend supported activities to another user after login.

### 11.2 Professional
A Professional user may assign or recommend supported activities to another user after login.

### 11.3 Logged-in Self User
A logged-in eligible user may click **Register Now** or **Try Now** and assign the activity to himself or herself.

### 11.4 Assignee / Individual
The Assignee is the person who receives the assigned activity and later sees it after login.

### 11.5 SuperAdmin
